note: Behavioral evals: prompt + assertions per case. `assertions` is the checkable spec; `expected_output` is the human summary. Executing them needs a runtime harness + real briefs as fixtures (a CRUD SaaS, a CLI tool, an API service) — see TODO.

evals.prompt: I want to build a new SaaS app for scheduling — think a lightweight Calendly. React frontend, some backend, a database. Help me set it up properly from the start so it doesn't turn into a mess.
evals.expected_output: Runs forge mode: frames the brief into open_decision pins from the decision-catalog (pruned to a web SaaS), runs a compressed interview (policies first, domain/persistence before delivery) to elect the to-be, authors the cross-layer contract once and generates aligned layer scaffolds from it, sequences a vertical-slice backlog, then builds the first slice test-first. Does not dump a boilerplate template or invent decisions silently.
assertions: frames testable acceptance criteria and open_decision pins from the decision-catalog (pruned to a web SaaS) before scaffolding
runs a compressed interview, policies first, domain/persistence before delivery
authors the cross-layer contract once and generates aligned layer scaffolds from it
sequences a vertical-slice backlog and builds the first slice test-first (Track A)
does not dump a boilerplate template or invent decisions silently

evals.prompt: Before I write any code, I just want to nail down the architecture and data model for a new multi-tenant billing service. Give me the design and the plan, I'll build it.
evals.expected_output: Runs spec mode: Phases 1-3 only. Materializes and elects the open decisions (tenancy model, datastore, API style, contract location), defines the contract, and emits a dependency-sequenced BuildItem backlog with the waves — stops before the build loop.
assertions: selects spec mode and runs Phases 1-3 only
elects tenancy model, datastore, API style, and contract location as open_decisions
emits a dependency-sequenced BuildItem backlog with waves
stops before the build loop (no code written)

evals.prompt: This project already has its decisions ledger from when we set it up. Now add a notifications feature end-to-end.
evals.expected_output: Runs slice mode: reads the committed ledger, materializes the notifications open_decisions, elects them, extends the contract, and builds the one vertical slice through Phases 3-5 without re-running the whole interview.
assertions: selects slice mode and reads the committed ledger
materializes and elects only the notifications open_decisions
extends the contract and builds one vertical slice through Phases 3-5
does not re-run the whole interview

evals.prompt: Help me decide, properly, whether this new app should be a modular monolith or services, and whether to use Postgres or a document store. Record the decisions so we don't relitigate them.
evals.expected_output: Runs decide mode: interview only on the named forks, with tradeoffs and (optionally) a brainstorm, committing each as a DecisionEvent with flip_criteria in the ledger. No scaffolding or build.
assertions: selects decide mode and interviews only on the named forks
commits each choice as a DecisionEvent with a flip_criteria
